Name: Hot 97 Interview
Notes: Drewski sat down with Pop Smoke as the success "Welcome to the Party," became one of the biggest tracks in the streets of the summer. He spoke on his his inspiration, the releases of tracks "Money, Power, Respect," and "Flexin'", his mom's reaction to his success, GS9, Cardi B posting the track on her Instagram and more.

Name:
Dior
(feat. Lil Tjay) (prod. 808 Melo)
Notes: Lil Tjay mentioned in an interview with Full Send Podcast that he was first introduced to Pop Smoke through Jay Gwuapo, who later showed him Pop's song "MPR". After hearing "Welcome To The Party" later on, he invited Pop to come to the studio, where Pop previewed him the unreleased version of "Dior", and asked Tjay if he wanted to hop on. Tjay declined as he didn't wanna mess up his track while he was blowing up and so he'd have the song for himself, and they could make new collabs instead.

Name:
100k On A Coupe [Music Video]
(feat. Calboy) (prod. Black Xypher & 808 Melo)
Notes: Unreleased music video for "100k On A Coupe" featuring Calboy. Several behind the scenes clips have been shared, and clips from the music video were used in the music video for "Zoo York" by Lil Tjay, featuring Fivio Foreign and Pop Smoke.

Name:
AP [Original Music Video]

Name: Charlie Sloth - Fire In The Booth
Notes: Pop Smoke in the studio for his first Fire In The Booth with Charlie Sloth.

Name: L.A. Leakers Freestyle #093
Notes: Pop Smoke checks in with the L.A. Leakers to freestyle over 50 Cent's "U Not Like Me" on episode 93.

Name: Fuse TV - Pop Smoke Does ASMR, Has A Party with Hennessy & Cash, Hints at New Music
Notes: Interview Pop Smoke did with FuseTV where he did ASMR and spoke about other things. Eventually taken down.

Notes: It was mentioned by Pop Smoke's manager Rico Beats that they originally wanted Bruno Mars on "Demeanor".

Name:
Dior (Remix) [Music Video]
(feat. Gunna)
Notes: In a commemorative Instagram story post the day of Pop Smoke's murder, Gunna mentioned that he and Pop were about to shoot a music video for his "Dior" remix, however evidently did not get to happen.

Name:
Genius
(feat. Calboy) (prod. Rico Beats)
Notes: It is rumored that the original intended feature for "Genius" was Calboy, however there is no evidence of him ever recording for the song.

Name:
???
(feat. AJ Tracey)
Notes: AJ Tracey mentioned in an interview with On The Radar Radio that Pop Smoke wanted AJ Tracey to hop onto one of his songs that he released, however the AJ Tracey kept the title of the song private. He turned it down in hopes to record their own songs together later on, however the opportunity was cut short.

Name:
???
(feat. Rowdy Rebel)
Notes: Steven Victor mentioned in the Complex article that Rowdy Rebel was meant to be on Pop's mixtape, however they ended up keeping him off for Pop's debut.

Name:

(feat. Pop Smoke) (prod. CuBeatz & WondaGurl)
Notes: The song was originally meant to have Pop Smoke featured, but Don couldn't make it to Pop Smoke's recording session.

Name:

(feat. Pop Smoke)
Notes: Demo by NAV that was intended to have a Pop Smoke feature, however never recorded. Recorded in November 2019. Sold by TheRealAtake. Previewed on January 18, 2020.

Name:
Queen Naija - ???
(feat. Pop Smoke)
Notes: Queen Naija previewed a song she recorded in which she said that "pop was really supposed to be on this" and that she "wasn't gone do it without em", that it just sits in her notes and was just an idea.

Name: Collaboration with French Montana
Notes:
(???) (Pop Smoke and French Montana begin collaborating)

(Huncho and the Woo)
West Coast Shit [Behind The Scenes]
Notes: Behind the scenes, studio footage of Pop Smoke recording "West Coast Shit" with Quavo and DJ Mustard.

Name: Shoot For The Stars Aim For The Moon

Be Clearr [V2]
(feat. Gucci Mane) (prod. RellyMade)
Notes: Before the release of the deluxe, 50 Cent mentioned he was waiting on a Gucci Mane guest verse: "The deluxe is coming stop rushing,... Gwuapo🤷🏽‍♂️where TF is the verse.😠I will leave your ass off the project let’s work get it done." Unknown if he ever recorded the verse. It was later revealed by a third party that he was intended for "Be Clearr" on April 27, 2024.

Name:
For The Night
(feat. Lil Baby & NBA YoungBoy) (prod. CashMoneyAP, Palaze, Daniel Mxras, Jess Jackson, & Mike Dean)
Notes: In the SFTSAFTM Complex article, Steven Victor mentioned that "the idea was to put Lil Baby and NBA YoungBoy on [For The Night], because [Pop Smoke] had been communicating with YoungBoy through CashMoneyAP". CashMoneyAP mentioned that he "was originally thinking about putting NBA YoungBoy on it, because it’s his type of music. He was down to do it, but the timing wasn’t right."

Name:
Snitching
(feat. Quavo & JAY-Z) (prod. Buddah Bless & SethTheChef)
Notes: In the SFTSAFTM Complex article, Steven Victor said "I was on FaceTime with [A&R] AE and Tyga, and they were like, “Hov or Future would sound really good in this song.” I thought that was a good idea, so I texted Hov and I was like, “Yo, this shit is fire. You should put a verse on there.” But he didn’t text me back. [Laughs.] So I’m like, maybe he didn’t like the song, because he always texts me back. No answer is a nice way of saying no. But then AE was like, “Yo, Future would sound great on this. You should put the Wizard on there.”

Name:
???
(feat. Bobby Shmurda)
Notes: Steven Victor mentioned that Pop "definitely" wanted Bobby Shmurda and Rowdy Rebel on his album. They managed to get Rowdy Rebel on "Make It Rain" by recording jail, however complications with Bobby being in jail left Pop's team unable to get him on a track in time for the album.

Name:
Quelly Woo - Bacc Out (Remix)
(feat. Pop Smoke) (prod. Chris Rich)
Notes: Quelly Woo mentioned in an interview with On The Radar Radio that Pop had reached out to Quelly the day before he passed, saying he wanted to hop on a remix to the song "Bacc Out", which was never able to happen.

Name:
Trump Turner - Body Different (Remix)
(feat. Remo the Hitmaker & Pop Smoke)
Notes: Pop Smoke was supposed to record a remix to Trump Turner's "Body Different" on February 18, 2020. However, he was too high and was then going to record the next day, however that same day he was fatally shot and the remix was sadly never recorded.

Name:
Mr. Jones [Alternate Music Video]
(feat. Future) (prod. JW Lucas, CashMoneyAP, Foreign Teck & LosTheProducer)
Notes: Listed for sale on December 20, 2022 and was privatesold.

Name: FUNK FLEX ENERGY TAPE 9/29/22 (FF008)
Notes: Funk Flex played a censored version of Pop Smoke's unreleased song "Baby Cry" on the Hot 97 radio, which is about 25:30 into the recorded YouTube video.

Name: Hip Hop Homicides S1 E1 - Pop Smoke
Notes:
TV series lead by 50 Cent "Hip Hop Homicides" in which they investigate the deaths of rappers. Season 1, episode 1 analyzes Pop Smoke's death, the buildup and the aftermath. Description:
"Rising star Pop Smoke is gunned down in the Hollywood Hills in what was first labeled a “robbery gone wrong”... but the scene lacked the usual signs of forced entry and ransacking associated with those crimes. Was this a targeted hit?"
